title: Rain-Soft Tokyo: Shiodome, Roppongi, Akasaka, Akihabara and Ariake

days.activities: Morning: arrive at Tokyo Narita at 07:05 on the overnight Scoot nonstop from Singapore, take the rail transfer into Shiodome/Shimbashi, drop bags at The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome, and ease into the day with coffee and a covered walk around Shiodome. Afternoon: head to Roppongi Hills for Grand Hyatt Tokyo as a polished indoor stop, treating it as an activity by lingering over its restaurants and elegant lobby-bar atmosphere, with optional spa-style downtime if access or a day package is available. Evening: stay in the Roppongi/Shimbashi corridor for a warming ramen dinner, then return to Shiodome for an early night after the flight.

stops.name: Hamarikyu Gardens
stops.description: Stroll the tidal pond and matcha teahouse beside Shiodome to ease off the overnight flight.

stops.name: Grand Hyatt Tokyo
stops.description: Linger over the Roppongi Hills hotel's restaurants and elegant lobby-bar atmosphere.

stops.name: Tokyo Midtown
stops.description: Wind down with dinner among Tokyo Midtown's restaurants a short walk from Roppongi.

days.hotel: The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome
days.narration: Tokyo greets you through a veil of light rain, all glossy pavements and station chimes after the red-eye from Singapore. By evening, the city feels softer: warm broth, hotel lights above Shiodome, and the relief of being exactly where the rail lines make everything easy.
days.weather_strategy: Light rain makes this an indoor-and-covered arrival day, with hotel dining, lobby-bar ambience, and short rail hops prioritized over outdoor exploring.

days.activities: Morning: start slowly from Shiodome with a covered rail ride to Akasaka and a reserved late-morning visit to Harry Potter Cafe at Akasaka Biz Tower, enjoying the themed dining rooms before the lunch rush. Afternoon: continue by train to Akihabara for SANDO LAB TOKYO at Don Quijote Akihabara 1F, pairing a crisp fried sandwich with an easy indoor browse through Akihabara’s electronics, anime, and game shops. Evening: return toward Shimbashi/Ginza for ramen or izakaya-style comfort food within walking distance of the hotel.

stops.name: Harry Potter Cafe
stops.description: Reserved themed dining rooms at Akasaka Biz Tower before the lunch rush.

stops.name: SANDO LAB TOKYO
stops.description: Crisp fried sandwich on Don Quijote Akihabara 1F, then browse the electronics district.

stops.name: Mandarake Complex
stops.description: Eight floors of anime and collectibles in Akihabara before a Ginza-side dinner.

days.hotel: The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome
days.narration: Rain taps at the city while you slip from spellbook interiors to neon shopfronts, Tokyo turning the weather into part of the mood. The day tastes like buttered toast, fried sando crunch, and a steaming bowl at night.
days.weather_strategy: Heavier rain is absorbed by indoor cafe, food, and shopping stops connected by efficient train routes.

days.activities: Morning: keep the umbrella handy and travel west to Kokubunji for Popo, verifying the exact venue before setting out and aiming for the 11:30–14:30 lunch window. Afternoon: return across town at an unhurried pace, using Shiodome as a reset point for rest, convenience-store snacks, or an indoor onsen-style bathhouse/spa option nearby if you want warm-water downtime. Evening: enjoy a walkable Shimbashi or Ginza dinner, favoring ramen counters or cozy restaurants close enough to retreat quickly if the rain strengthens.

stops.name: Popo
stops.description: Aim for the 11:30-14:30 lunch window in Kokubunji, confirming the venue before setting out.

stops.name: Ginza Six
stops.description: Return east for the rooftop garden and depachika food hall at Ginza Six.

stops.name: Caretta Shiodome
stops.description: Sky-high dinner at Caretta Shiodome's 46th-floor restaurants beside the hotel.

days.hotel: The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome
days.narration: This is the slow Tokyo day: train windows streaked with rain, a small restaurant search, and the satisfaction of finding warmth at the end of the line. Back near Shiodome, the city compresses into lanterns, steam, and the easy rhythm of walking home.
days.weather_strategy: Because rain continues, the farthest and most ambiguous food stop is scheduled as a lunch mission, while the rest of the day stays flexible and close to transit.

days.activities: Morning: check out of The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome, store luggage if needed, and ride to Ariake for Tokyo Dream Park, saving this theme-park-style entertainment complex for the lightest-rain day of the trip. Afternoon: focus on its indoor Doraemon exhibition areas, rotating 7th–8th floor event spaces, event-limited goods, and, if the drizzle eases, a short visit to the free 6th-floor rooftop terrace with Doraemon figures. Evening: return to Shiodome/Shimbashi to collect luggage, have a final ramen or casual dinner if timing allows, and begin onward airport or next-destination logistics.

stops.name: Tokyo Dream Park
stops.description: Indoor Doraemon exhibition and rooftop terrace, saved for the lightest-rain day.

stops.name: teamLab Planets TOKYO
stops.description: Immersive digital-art world a short Yurikamome ride away in Toyosu.

stops.name: Ariake Garden
stops.description: Final dinner and onsen-style downtime at Ariake Garden before airport logistics.

days.narration: The last day opens like a bright comic panel despite the drizzle, with Doraemon blues and rooftop air above Ariake. Tokyo sends you off playfully: one more train ride, one more bowl, one more glimpse of wet streets shining under evening lights.
days.weather_strategy: Tokyo Dream Park is placed on the lighter-rain departure day because its indoor exhibits can handle showers, while the rooftop terrace is kept optional for any clearer break.

recommended_hotel: The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome
recommended_flight: Scoot nonstop economy, Singapore SIN 23:05 → Tokyo Narita NRT 07:05 +1 on 2026-06-10, about 7h; surfaced fare about USD 190.90 one-way in live search, with Scoot direct June round-trip fares shown from about SGD 543 subject to availability.

places.name: Tokyo Dream Park
places.summary: New Ariake entertainment complex opened March 27, 2026. Highlights include rotating event spaces on the 7th–8th floors, Doraemon-themed exhibition areas such as the Mirror Room, Colorful Doraemon Corridor, Nobita's Manga Room, event-limited goods, and a free-to-access 6th-floor rooftop terrace with Doraemon figures. Tip: check the official site before visiting for current ticketing, hours, closed days, and prices.

places.name: Grand Hyatt Tokyo
places.summary: Luxury hotel in Roppongi Hills, Minato-ku. Useful highlights for a stay include multiple dining options, Grand Club lounge services, and Nagomi Spa & Fitness access for eligible rooms. Tip: best for travelers who want a polished Roppongi base with restaurants, nightlife, museums, and shopping nearby; confirm package inclusions before booking.

places.name: Harry Potter Cafe
places.summary: Official themed cafe at Akasaka Biz Tower, 1F. Listed hours: cafe 10:00–17:00, dinner 17:00–22:00, and takeout Harry Potter Cafe Window 11:00–21:00, with last order 30 minutes before closing. Tip: advance reservations are recommended for the cafe; same-day phone reservations are not accepted.

places.name: SANDO LAB TOKYO
places.summary: Akihabara fried-sandwich shop built around the concept of reinventing Japanese sandwiches; signature fried sandwiches are described as crisp outside and fluffy inside. Location: Don Quijote Akihabara 1F, near Akihabara and Suehirocho stations. Listed hours: weekdays 10:30–21:30, holidays/weekends 10:00–21:45. Tip: good casual stop while walking Akihabara.

places.name: Popo
places.summary: Search results matched 'popo cafe' in Kokubunji, Tokyo, listed as a Japanese restaurant. Listed hours: lunch 11:30–14:30 and dinner 17:00–23:00. Tip: because 'Popo' is ambiguous in Tokyo search results, verify this is the intended venue before routing the itinerary.

hotel_options.name: The Royal Park Hotel Iconic Tokyo Shiodome
hotel_options.cancellation_policy: Free cancellation until 48 hours before check-in
hotel_options.price_summary: Mid-range to upper-mid. Direct-access Shiodome tower with strong rail links to Narita and walkable Ginza/Shimbashi.
hotel_options.rationale: Best pick: meets every requirement — central Shiodome base with covered station access (great for the rainy arrival days), walking-friendly to Ginza/Shimbashi dining, and an easy NRT transfer that fits the Scoot arrival.
tradeoffs: Not the cheapest option in the shortlist.
Shiodome is business-district quiet at night versus livelier Shinjuku/Shibuya.

hotel_options.name: Park Hotel Tokyo (Shiodome)
hotel_options.price_summary: Mid-range. Art-themed rooms in the Shiodome Media Tower, same walkable cluster.
hotel_options.rationale: Strong runner-up in the same base: similar Shiodome convenience and Ginza walkability at a slightly more design-forward, sometimes lower price point.
tradeoffs: Rooms can be smaller than the best pick.
Higher floors fill fast on popular dates.

hotel_options.name: Conrad Tokyo (Shiodome)
hotel_options.price_summary: Luxury. Bayfront Shiodome tower with spa and skyline views.
hotel_options.rationale: Comfort/upgrade option in the same base for travelers willing to spend more: larger rooms, spa, and premium dining while keeping the identical Shiodome transit advantages.
tradeoffs: Well above the mid-range budget for this trip.
Premium amenities are nice-to-have, not required by the stated preferences.

weather_adjustments.reason: Tokyo Dream Park includes a rooftop terrace and theme-park-style elements, so it was placed on a lighter-rain day rather than the heavier-rain days.
Indoor cafe and shopping-friendly stops were grouped on a rainier day to reduce weather exposure.
